sub: 340,784 tokens the agent never had to re-read last week, across 91 doc-lookups. our own fleet run.

headline: two layers, one the agent can't rewrite

cols.head: AGENT RULES
cols.body: editable. the agent tunes these as it works.

cols.head: SOURCE OF TRUTH
cols.body: read-only. a layer the agent can't rewrite.

headline: first 10 minutes with a new agent
sub: don't hand it the repo. hand it 3 files and one query.

headline: two of my agents edited past each other today
sub: they didn't disagree on the work. they disagreed on which doc was current.

headline: the runtime piece nobody lists
sub: memory, tools, eval. everyone names those. the one nobody lists is current: which doc is true right now.

headline: 19% slower, felt 20% faster
sub: METR randomized controlled trial: experienced developers completed tasks 19% slower with early-2025 AI tools, while estimating AI made them 20% faster.

headline: the model is the easy part
sub: the hard part of shipping agents isn't the model, it's the runtime around it: current context, review gates, a rollback path.

headline: stale context is a reliability problem, not a token problem
sub: an agent acting confidently on a 3-week-old doc ships the wrong call. the cost lands in production, not the bill.
